name: JRiver Media Center
description: JRiver Media Center is a comprehensive media management application for audio, video, images, and television. This LXC runs MC35 on Debian 13 with browser-based noVNC access over HTTPS, native RDP, and GPU-backed Sunshine streaming for Moonlight clients, while Media Server mode runs by default for more fault-tolerant daily use.

notes.text: A JRiver Media Center license is required. Purchase at https://www.jriver.com/
Open https://CONTAINER_IP:5800/setup/ to create the initial web credentials for the JRMC Dashboard and noVNC access. The HTTPS certificate is self-signed.
The container starts in Media Server mode. Use the Dashboard to launch the full JRMC UI only when interactive administration is needed.
Dashboard now exposes four primary modes: Media Server, VNC, RDP, and Sunshine. Choosing one mode cleanly tears down the others first so JRiver keeps one writable library owner.

VNC mode enables both browser noVNC and direct VNC on port 5900 at the same time against one shared JRMC session through one TurboVNC backend. After Dashboard sign-in, use the same Dashboard password when noVNC or a native VNC client prompts for VNC authentication.

Sunshine mode is intended for GPU-enabled installs. Switch into Sunshine from Dashboard first, then open Sunshine Status or Mode Status to reach Sunshine's admin UI on port 47990, create Sunshine's separate admin account, and pair Moonlight.

Dashboard includes 100%-200% JRMC UI scale presets. In VNC mode, larger presets request a larger server framebuffer so noVNC can scale it back down cleanly while JRiver keeps its own internal Standard View size at 1.
In RDP mode, the same presets are translated into JRiver's internal Standard View size before launch: 100%->1, 125%->1.25, 150%->1.5, 175%->1.75, and 200%->2.

Dashboard can also route JRiver audio to a trusted-LAN Linux PipeWire host by targeting its pipewire-pulse TCP listener. Provide the host/IP, optional port, and optional sink name from the Dashboard Remote Audio card.
After enabling remote audio and restarting JRiver, the preferred Linux output device inside JRiver is pulse.

GPU passthrough can be enabled during installation for this LXC. Inside the container, use jrmc-gpu-status and jrmc-gpu-test to verify device visibility, FFmpeg acceleration backends, and vendor-specific probes such as vainfo or nvidia-smi.
Remote host setup requires PipeWire plus pipewire-pulse listening on a trusted LAN address such as tcp:4713, then choosing a playback sink from `pactl list short sinks`.
Run `jrmc-configure` for local administration, `jrmc-mode` to switch between Media Server, VNC, RDP, and Sunshine modes, and `jrmc-activate /path/to/file.mjr` to import a license restore file.

Installation uses the installJRMC community script (https://github.com/cryobry/installJRMC) for JRiver package installation and updates, while this LXC manages noVNC, web access, and service mode switching locally.
